message:
feat(web): integrate WASM dot plot with progressive refinement

Dot Plot Worker (dotplot.worker.ts):
- Add WASM acceleration via dotplot_self_buffers when available
- Implement progressive refinement: send low-res preview (40 bins) first,
  then full resolution, for perceived performance improvement
- Support both string sequences and SequenceBytesRef for zero-copy transfer
- Add job cancellation via activeJobId to prevent stale results
- Transfer Float32Array buffers to main thread for efficiency
- Fall back to JS computeDotPlot when WASM unavailable or fails
- Add helper functions: tryComputeDotPlotWasm, getSequenceBytesForWasm,
  getSequenceBytesView, decodeSequenceRef, decodeAsciiBytes

DotPlotOverlay (DotPlotOverlay.tsx):
- Use SharedSequencePool.getOrCreateRef for worker communication
- Transfer sequence buffers with postMessage for efficiency
- Import DotPlotWorkerResponse type from centralized types.ts
- Fall back to string path for unknown phage IDs (compatibility)

WASM Parity Tests (dotplot-wasm-parity.test.ts):
- Add comprehensive parity tests between WASM and JS implementations
- Test cases: basic ACGT, real phage sequences, edge cases (single base,
  repeated sequences, N characters)
- Verify bins, window, direct, and inverted values match within tolerance
- Use flattenCore helper to convert nested grid to Float32Array for comparison
- Tests ensure WASM optimization doesn't change results

Performance: WASM dot plot is significantly faster for large genomes,
and progressive refinement provides instant visual feedback while
full resolution computes in background.

Refs: phage_explorer-8qk2.6, phage_explorer-ef852fa
